chapter_title: The Kite That Found Morning Light
text: On the edge of Maple Hill stood a tiny workshop with blue windows and a silver roof that flashed in the sun. Inside, Lina bent over a workbench covered in gears, string, feathers, and little brass screws. She was building the finest kite the town had ever seen. But this was no ordinary kite. Its frame was made of willow wood that bent without breaking, its wings were stitched from bright orange cloth with swirls of gold, and at its center Lina placed a clockwork heart no bigger than an apple. When she wound it three careful turns, it gave a soft, cheerful tick. That afternoon she carried the kite to the top of the hill, where the wind ran laughing through the grass. The kite leaped into the sky, circled once, and then tugged hard toward the dark woods beyond town as if it had somewhere important to go. Curious, Lina followed the humming string down a narrow path until the kite dipped beside a thorny blackberry bush. Hidden beneath the leaves was a small wooden box carved with stars. Inside lay a note and a handful of glowing glass marbles, each one shining a different color. The note said the marbles were called the Marbles of Morning and that they should be shared whenever the town felt gloomy. That evening Lina placed them on the fountain in the town square. Red light danced across the cobblestones, blue shimmered on the bakery windows, green glowed on the trees, and warm gold spilled across the smiling faces of neighbors who had forgotten how much joy a little wonder could bring. Children twirled through the colors, the baker gave away sweet buns, and even grumpy Mr. Thorne laughed out loud. Lina looked at her marvelous kite resting beside her and heard its little heart tick once, happily, as if to say the town was bright again.
